'''Edward "Eddie" Battye''' (born 30 December 1991) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Hunslet RLFC|Hunslet]].

He has previously played for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] over three separate spells in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], the [[Villeneuve Leopards]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] and the [[London Broncos]] in the Championship and the [[Super League]]. Battye has also spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Battye was born in England. He was brought up on a buffalo farm in [[Penistone]], [[South Yorkshire]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=London Broncos' Eddie Battye goes from buffalo farmer to Super League |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/feb/09/london-broncos-eddie-battaye-always-believed-good-enough-super-league |work=The Guardian |date=9 February 2019 |accessdate=21 February 2019}}</ref>

==Career==
===Sheffield Eagles===
Battye played junior rugby league for [[Hillsborough Hawks]] and signed with the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and made his [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] debut in 2011.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

After a spell in France playing for [[Villeneuve Leopards]] he returned to the Eagles and became a regular, featuring in their Championship winning team in 2013. He joined [[London Broncos]] in 2016.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

In 2017, Battye was kept out of rugby league for four months due to a knee injury.

===London Broncos===
He put in a man of the match performance in the first game of the 2018 Qualifiers against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] at The Halton stadium. His team [[London Broncos]] came out 20–21 winners in an enthralling game at The Halton stadium in Widnes.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Battye put in an outstanding performance and contributed a try in an outstanding team effort to defeat [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in an 18-16 thriller on 9 March 2019.

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 24 January 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=24 Jan 2021|accessdate=24 Jan 2021|title=Eddie Battye: Wakefield Trinity sign London Broncos prop|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55790529}}</ref>
Battye featured in 24 games for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles (re-join)===
On 2 Nov 2023 it was reported that he will re-join [[Sheffield Eagles]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=2 Nov 2023|title=Eddie Battye enjoys homecoming with next destination confirmed for ex-Wakefield Trinity man|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/eddie-battye-enjoys-sheffield-eagles-homecoming-with-next-destination-confirmed-for-ex-wakefield-trinity-man}}</ref>

===Hunslet RLFC===
On 11 October 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Sheffield Eagles]] and joined [[Hunslet RLFC]] on a one-year deal for 2026<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Veteran ex-Super League stalwart seals cross-Championship switch ahead of 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/eddie-battye-joins-hunslet-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>
